skill.name: constitutional-writer
skill.description: Extracts mission, values, governance, decision-making, and quality standards from source documents, then formats them into a project constitution.
skill.summary: Turn project documents into a structured constitution.
skill.author: Azeem-2

security_audit.summary: Static findings for weak cryptography, network reconnaissance, certificate files, and shell execution are false positives caused by constitution-writing terminology and Markdown examples. The skill includes a Python helper that reads a user-supplied local text file, which is legitimate for extraction but creates a low filesystem/script risk.

low_findings.title: Local File Reading Helper Script
low_findings.description: The helper script accepts a path from the command line and reads that local file before extracting constitutional content. This supports the skill purpose, but users should only run it on intended documents.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read is explicit and reachable through the command-line argument. I found no network transmission or secret exfiltration, so the risk remains low.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Terms
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings occur in descriptive constitution text and regex phrases such as purpose, state, principles, authority, and accountability. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or key handling is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language document guidance or extraction keywords. They do not implement or configure cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance finding is the phrase about measuring success in a reference document. It does not call network tools, enumerate hosts, or open connections.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The location is a documentation definition under Quality Standards. No network-related code or command appears there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Certificate Or Key File Indicators
low_findings.description: The certificate and key findings are extraction categories, keyword dictionaries, and loop logic in the Python helper. They are not certificate files, private keys, or credential material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script contains only text extraction patterns and category iteration at these lines. I found no embedded certificates, private keys, or secret values.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Treated As Execution
low_findings.description: The shell execution findings are Markdown formatting for tags, a sample constitution block, and tool names. They do not execute commands and do not interpolate user input into a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown code spans and code fences in documentation. There is no Ruby code, shell call, or command execution primitive in SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: Teams often bury mission, values, governance, and quality standards across scattered documents. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code extract those elements and format a clear project constitution.
actual_capabilities: Identifies mission, purpose, vision, values, governance, and quality standards in source documents.
Formats extracted material into a structured project constitution.
Uses a reusable Markdown constitution template for consistent sections.
Provides reference guidance for common constitutional elements and document types.
Includes a Python helper for extracting constitutional text from local text files.
limitations: It is not legal advice and does not create legally binding bylaws.
It depends on source documents that already contain relevant constitutional information.
The helper script reads plain text files and does not parse PDF or DOCX files by itself.
It may miss implied values or informal governance rules that are not written clearly.

use_cases.title: Draft a Project Constitution
use_cases.description: Convert a charter, strategy brief, or planning document into a concise constitution.

use_cases.title: Standardize Governance Notes
use_cases.description: Extract decision rights, accountability, and escalation paths from existing documentation.

use_cases.title: Prepare Team Alignment Material
use_cases.description: Create a shared summary of mission, values, standards, and cultural commitments.

prompt_templates.title: Extract a Basic Constitution
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this project document and extract only constitutional content. Format the result with mission, vision, values, governance, decisions, standards, and culture sections.

prompt_templates.title: Consolidate Multiple Sources
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these source documents and create one project constitution. Remove duplicates, preserve clear source wording, and do not add unsupported claims.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Governance
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract governance, decision-making, accountability, and escalation details from this document. Present them as constitution sections with concise bullet points.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Constitution Completeness
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare this draft constitution against the source documents. Identify missing mission, values, governance, standards, or culture content without inventing new material.

output_examples.input: A project charter with purpose, team values, decision rules, and quality goals.
output_examples.output: A structured constitution with sections for Mission and Purpose, Vision, Core Values, Governance, Decision Making, Quality Standards, and Cultural Commitments.

output_examples.input: Several planning notes that repeat similar principles in different wording.
output_examples.output: A consolidated constitution that removes duplicate statements and keeps the clearest supported wording.

output_examples.input: A governance memo focused on authority, responsibility, and escalation.
output_examples.output: A constitution excerpt that separates governance structure, decision rights, accountability, and escalation paths.

best_practices: Provide source documents with explicit mission, values, governance, and quality language.
Ask the skill to preserve exact wording when source language is important.
Review the final constitution with stakeholders before treating it as official guidance.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to invent values or governance rules that are absent from the source.
Do not use it as a substitute for legal review of formal bylaws or policies.
Do not run the helper script on files that contain unrelated secrets or private data.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a structured project constitution from existing written material.

faq.question: Can it work with more than one document?
faq.answer: Yes. It can consolidate related documents when they are provided in the conversation or readable workspace.

faq.question: Does it provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It supports documentation and alignment, not legal drafting or legal interpretation.

faq.question: What sections does it look for?
faq.answer: It looks for mission, vision, values, principles, governance, decisions, quality standards, and culture.

faq.question: Can it process PDFs directly?
faq.answer: The skill instructions mention PDF handling through another PDF skill. The included Python helper only reads text files.

faq.question: How should I review the output?
faq.answer: Check that every statement is supported by the source documents and confirm governance language with responsible stakeholders.
